title: Local Clusters, Cluster Lifecycle, Conformance, Upgrades, Backup, and Production Defense · Stakeholder Platform Mission
summary: An engineering, operations, accessibility, and security review board. The board needs a rendered immutable application release, tested rollout and scaling, observability, policy, extension trust, supported upgrade, control-plane and application recovery drills, accessible runbook, and defended transfer limits. Planned artifact: Deliver Production Kubernetes Release and Recovery Defense with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress th…
objectives: Test load, Pod loss, node drain, zone loss, slow dependency, scaling lag, quota, and recovery while measuring user-visible service objectives.
Diagnose desired state, admission, scheduling, image, mount, process, probe, endpoint, DNS, policy, routing, storage, node, and application layers using the least invasive evidence.
Design pull-based reconciliation, field ownership, drift policy, secret authority, immutable promotion, health gates, rollback, and break-glass recovery without vendor coupling.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Project K8s Capacity Chaos Recovery Evidence
steps.instruction:
project case — A project board plans a 1.35 to 1.36 control-plane upgrade with skewed kubelets, deprecated APIs, expired recovery assumptions, untested etcd restore, and unclear rollback. Defend compatibility, drain, backup, staged upgrade, recovery, and ownership.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Project K8s Capacity Chaos Recovery Evidence.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for test load, pod loss, node drain, zone loss, slow dependency, scaling lag, quota, and recovery while measuring user-visible service objectives. steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Project K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone
steps.instruction:
Append the Kubernetes evidence item headed "  # Evidence: k8s-disaster-recovery-capstone-hx630u". Tailor its cluster-recovery-defense.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic API or manifest review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run kubectl, Helm, kubeadm, and cluster commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Project K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Project K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone, keep K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-disaster-recovery-capstone-hx630u
  - competency: k8s-disaster-recovery-capstone
    artifact: cluster-recovery-defense.yaml
    decision: disaster recovery capstone
    environment: air-gapped release cluster using an internal registry mirror
    fault-injection: restore a claim in a different topology and numeric identity
    retained-proof: component versions skew drain timing and recovery rehearsal
    case-key: hx630u
    change: |
      baseline: kubernetes-1.36.2
      local-proof: minikube-1.38.1
      distribution: supported-and-conformant
      upgrade: patch-current-then-next-minor
      order: api-server-then-controllers-then-kubelet
      drain: capacity-and-pdb-verified
      etcd: encrypted-snapshot-restored
      application-state: independently-restored
      rto-rpo: rehearsed
    verify: kubectl get nodes -o json && kubeadm upgrade plan && etcdctl snapshot status backup.db
    failure: Reject minikube as production proof, skipped minor upgrades, kubelet-first order, drain without capacity, etcd-only recovery, or backup without restore.
    repair: Confirm support and skew, scan removed APIs, canary components and workloads, preserve keys and state, rehearse restore order, and verify user service.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Defend control-plane and application backups, etcd snapshots, encryption keys, manifests, external state, restore order, DNS and traffic cutover, recovery objectives, drills, and residual risk.

hints: For Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone, name the context, namespace, API identity, desired state, observed status, controller, policy, and failure-domain boundary, K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone, trace this concrete input and predict admission, defaulting, scheduling, reconciliation, status, traffic, storage, policy, disruption, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A project board plans a 1.35 to 1.36 control-plane upgrade with skewed kubelets, deprecated APIs, expired recovery assumptions, untested etcd restore, and unclear rollback. Defend compatibility, drain, backup, staged upgrade, recovery, and ownership. Competency probe: Defend control-plane and application backups, etcd snapshots, encryption keys, manifests, external state, restore order, DNS and traffic cutover, recovery objectives, drills, and residual risk.

For Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone, challenge the documented misconception with object, condition, event, policy, failure, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: An etcd snapshot alone restores every application volume, external dependency, credential, and user-visible service.
